communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Massachusetts Public Records Law, I hereby request the following records:

Members of the Plympton Board of Health,

Pursuant to the Massachusetts Public Records Law, M.G.L. c.66 §10, I am requesting the following public records held by the Plympton Board of Health:

**Records sought:**

A complete tabular log (CSV or spreadsheet preferred) of every application for a Title 5 subsurface sewage disposal system variance, special permit, or local-regulation waiver received by the Plympton Board of Health from **January 1, 2020 to the date of this request**, including but not limited to **applications for mounded systems** and **applications under Plympton's local five-acre lot requirement for mounded systems**.

For each entry, please include:

- Date received
- Applicant name and the parcel address
- Assessor's Map / Block / Lot identification
- Lot size in acres (and whether the parcel was assembled from multiple deeded lots, if applicable)
- Type of variance / special permit / waiver sought (specific local rule cited)
- BoH decision (granted, granted with conditions, denied, withdrawn, continued)
- Date of decision
- Vote tally (e.g., 3-0, 2-1, with names of voting members)
- Any conditions imposed
- Whether the BoH recused any member
- Whether the matter was subsequently litigated or appealed

If a single comprehensive log does not exist, the underlying minutes and decision documents for each application requested are sufficient.

**Preferred format:** Electronic delivery (CSV or PDF) via email.

**Fee waiver:** I respectfully request a waiver of any fees under M.G.L. c.66 §10(d)(iv) and 950 CMR 32.07(2)(k). The records concern the consistency of Plympton Board of Health enforcement of its mounded-system regulation — a matter of significant public interest given (a) Plympton Superior Court Civil Action No. 2283CV00462 (*O'Hearn v. Town of Plympton*), (b) public statements regarding selective enforcement of the mounded-system rule, and (c) the contested Board of Health election on May 16, 2026.

**Response window:** Per M.G.L. c.66 §10(b), ten business days.

Thank you.

I also request that, if appropriate, fees be waived as we believe this request is in the public interest, as suggested but not stipulated by the recommendations of the Massachusetts Supervisor of Public Records. The requested documents will be made available to the general public free of charge as part of the public information service at MuckRock.com, and is not made for commercial usage.

I expect the request to be filled in an accessible format, including for screen readers, which provide text-to-speech for persons unable to read print. Files that are not accessible to screen readers include, for example, .pdf image files as well as physical documents.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Edward Havey

To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Massachusetts Public Records Law, I hereby request the following records:

Pursuant to M.G.L. c. 66 §10, I respectfully request:

1. All written disclosures filed by Town Manager Derek Brindisi under M.G.L. c. 268A §19(b)(1) from March 2022 to the present.
2. All written determinations issued by the Select Board under M.G.L. c. 268A §19(b)(1) regarding any disclosure filed by Mr. Brindisi during that period.
3. Specifically, any §19(b)(1) disclosure and determination concerning Mr. Brindisi's directorship of the Plymouth Regional Economic Development Foundation Inc and his participation as Town Manager in matters affecting that Foundation, including but not limited to the May 12, 2026 sale of the Joseph Simes House under Bid 22515.

If any portion is exempt, please identify the specific exemption claimed and produce the segregable portions. Per M.G.L. c. 66 §10, please respond within 10 business days.

I also request that, if appropriate, fees be waived as we believe this request is in the public interest, as suggested but not stipulated by the recommendations of the Massachusetts Supervisor of Public Records. The requested documents will be made available to the general public free of charge as part of the public information service at MuckRock.com, and is not made for commercial usage.

I expect the request to be filled in an accessible format, including for screen readers, which provide text-to-speech for persons unable to read print. Files that are not accessible to screen readers include, for example, .pdf image files as well as physical documents.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Edward Havey

To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Massachusetts Public Records Law, I hereby request the following records:

Pursuant to M.G.L. c. 66 §10, I respectfully request the following public records associated with Bid 22515 (Sale of Joseph Simes House, 27 and 29 Manomet Point Road):

1. The written justification memorandum required by M.G.L. c. 30B §16(g) for the acceptance of a bid below assessed value, signed by the Town Manager or other chief procurement officer.
2. All evaluation worksheets, scoring sheets, and committee recommendations associated with Bid 22515.
3. The complete Planholders List for Bid 22515 including any entity (such as the Plymouth Regional Economic Development Foundation Inc) that registered to bid.
4. The May 12, 2026 Select Board agenda packet, including all supporting documentation distributed to Select Board members in connection with the ratification of the Simes House sale.
5. The original Request for Proposals (RFP 22515) and any addenda issued.

If any portion is exempt, please identify the specific exemption claimed and produce the segregable portions. Per M.G.L. c. 66 §10, please respond within 10 business days.

I also request that, if appropriate, fees be waived as we believe this request is in the public interest, as suggested but not stipulated by the recommendations of the Massachusetts Supervisor of Public Records. The requested documents will be made available to the general public free of charge as part of the public information service at MuckRock.com, and is not made for commercial usage.

I expect the request to be filled in an accessible format, including for screen readers, which provide text-to-speech for persons unable to read print. Files that are not accessible to screen readers include, for example, .pdf image files as well as physical documents.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Edward Havey
